title: Terebinthus microphylla
intro: Terebinthus microphylla, also known as the Judean myrtle, is a small, shrub-like evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in relieving respiratory issues and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Terebinthus microphylla include essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into tea infusions, capsules, and ointments to treat various health conditions, such as bronchitis and eczema.
